premise: When my mother was diagnosed with stage-four cancer, I emptied my $130,000 retirement account to pay for an experimental treatment abroad while my sister refused to contribute a dime. Mom recovered fully — and then secretly rewrote her will to leave the $360,000 family home entirely to my sister, who evicted me right after the funeral; I later discovered a hidden codicil and a diary proving she manipulated Mom into changing the will, sued for tortious interference, and won a judgment that forced her to sell the house and pay me $275,000.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Mom Left Me Homeless After I Paid For Her Cancer. So I Watched Her Lose The H
